response:
Short answer: azr-compliance-v3 runs bulk Event Grid remediation as a server‑side, Postgres-backed, recoverable batch worker (not the existing sync_queue) that snapshots deployable targets, persists per-item state for UI continuity, and uses a single 15‑second timer worker guarded by an advisory lock with retries/requeue logic and Fleet/APIM polling knobs for scale and robustness.

Key details:
- Data model: two Postgres tables — event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items — store run metadata and per‑subscription item state so progress survives page refreshes and process restarts.
- Trigger and snapshotting: the bulk start endpoint will reuse an existing active run if present; when starting a run it snapshots the full deployable subscription set from the shared Event Grid coverage SQL rather than computing deployable targets repeatedly.
- Worker model: a dedicated ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation timer worker runs on a 15‑second cadence and is protected by a Postgres advisory lock so only one worker drives a run at a time.
- Recovery and robustness: stale/running items are requeued back to pending so worker restarts recover interrupted work; a recency index (migration added) and ordering by updated_at ensure latest items surface correctly.
- Fleet / APIM integration: the backend can acquire a ClientSecretCredential and call the Fleet/APIM remediation endpoints, polling the returned statusUrl until completion (backend polling behavior has dedicated env knobs). The frontend now calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure rather than calling Fleet directly in production scenarios where the backend must hold app credentials.
- Operational hardening: fixes to avoid recomputing deployable targets when an active run exists, to surface run state from a single paged query, and to make the UI use a read/refocus action for existing runs.

Together these choices make bulk Event Grid remediation scalable, observable, and resilient to worker restarts and long‑running external calls.

Implemented dedicated server-side Event Grid bulk remediation in azr-compliance-v3. Design choice: do not reuse sync_queue; instead use Postgres-backed tables event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items plus a dedicated 15-second timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation guarded by its own advisory lock. Bulk start endpoint reuses an active queued/running run, snapshots the full deployable subscription set from shared Event Grid coverage SQL, and persists latest-run item statuses for page refresh continuity. EventGridDetails now calls POST /api/event-grid/reme", "2026-04-09: azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediation was switched from a browser-side Fleet API call to a backend-mediated flow because Fleet requires application permission. The backend now acquires a ClientSecretCredential token for scope api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default and calls https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription, polling the returned statusUrl until completion. The frontend calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure instead of Fleet directly.
[specifics: service: azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediat", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, integrated Event Grid remediation on the Event Grid Details page as a browser-side Fleet Platform call rather than a backend proxy because the browser reliably has the operator MSAL Bearer token while the backend, behind APIM, may not retain the raw Authorization credential in deployed environments. The page now uses metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi), requests an optional Fleet-specific scope via VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE, calls POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFOR", "2026-04-10: PR #76 review feedback on server-side Event Grid bulk remediation produced four concrete follow-up fixes: avoid computing deployable targets when an active bulk run already exists; order latest bulk remediation items by recency instead of ascending id; source the BulkRemediationPanel run/loading state from the same paged query as its items to avoid mixed-run rendering; and make the 'View Active Bulk Run' button a true read/refocus action instead of issuing another POST.
[specifics: pr_number: 76; component: BulkRemediationPanel; followup_fix_1: avoid computing deployable targets when an active", "2026-04-10: Created follow-up hardening for Event Grid bulk remediation after PR #77 review comments. The fix adds forward migration 58 for a recency index on event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items(run_id, updated_at DESC, id DESC), adds bulk-worker-specific Fleet polling env knobs, and requeues stale running items back to pending so a worker restart can recover interrupted runs.
[specifics: pr_number: 77; forward_migration: 58; recency_index: event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items(run_id, updated_at DESC, id DESC); bulk_worker_fleet_polling_env_knobs: added (bulk-worker-specific Fleet polling env knobs); r", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, redesigned Event Grid Details to distinguish deployment inventory from remediation coverage. GetEventGridDeployments now returns metadata.coverage with deployable_missing_event_grid_count, missing_monitoring_count, and arrays for deployable_subscriptions vs out_of_scope_subscriptions, derived from active customer subscriptions, monitoring entitlement, and Rackspace-managed Event Grid inventory. The frontend EventGridDetails page now shows explicit remediation buckets: 'Ready For Event Grid Deployment' for monitoring-enabled subscriptions missing Event Grid, ", "2026-03-27: On 2026-03-27, live prod validation after a large event burst in azr-compliance-v3 showed the register path and queue handoff were working, but sync_bulk_automation_execute was delayed behind serial queue processing until 21:07Z. After it ran, post-21:07 transport was clean: /api/bulk-operations/register returned 201 four times, /api/bulk-operations/callback returned 200 seven times, and there were zero response 500s in api_audit_all for that post-21:07 window. Bulk item state since 19:30Z then settled to a mix of succeeded, invalid, queued, and failed: policy duplicates were suppressed as inv", "2026-03-30: On 2026-03-30 in azr-compliance-v3 develop, corrected a bad bulk-operations UI fix. The user did not want parent policy invalid/failed diagnostics copied to child alert rows. Removed the child-alert inheritance changes from web-dashboard/src/pages/BulkOperations.jsx and instead added backend backfill in backend-py/GetBulkOperationItems/__init__.py so invalid policy rows with blank last_error derive a reason from subscription_compliance_comparison and azure_policy_assignments. Added integration test test_get_bulk_operation_items_backfills_invalid_policy_reason and pushed commit ac0fc372 to deve", "2026-03-27: On 2026-03-27 in azr-compliance-v3 develop, fixed two bulk automation issues. First, RegisterBulkOperation was inserting compatibility rows as status=sent with selected=false and never queueing sync_bulk_automation_execute; changed it to queue status, selected=true, and immediate enqueue with self-healing sync operation registration. Second, ExecuteBulkOperation now validates policy create items before publish: it blocks create when current local comparison no longer needs remediation, when the assignment already exists with no current gap, or when a recent successful policy/AMA create tracker"]}
